input.title: Context View: Hash matched contexts do not show children
input.body:
## Steps to Reproduce
- a
  - cats [cursor, context view]
- b
  - cat
    - x

## Current Behavior
When the cursor view is activated on `a.cats`, it correctly shows both contexts, but does not ~show the child `x` under `b`~ allow the cursor to be set on `b/cat~/x`. 

## Expected Behavior
`b/cat~/x` should be selectable. It work exactly the same as when the values are an exact match:

- a
  - cat
- b
  - cat
    - x

## Notes
- It has to do with the fact that `cat` and `cats` correctly hash match (i.e. `hashThought('cat') === hashThought('cats')`), but the cursor logic is not using the correct `value` in this case.
- I am not sure if the problem is in the [generation of the `thoughtsRanked` value for `setCursor`](https://github.com/cybersemics/em/blob/dev/src/components/Editable.tsx#L128-L149), or in `setCursor` itself, so I am not sure yet where the proper unit test belongs.
